rulings.Case Title: PETER M OWENS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Why Respondent Shall Not Be Held In Comtept For Failure To Comply With The Writ
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2022 line 1. HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is DENIED. The writ issued by this Court directed the respondents to "set aside your decision denying Petitioners' Application and approve that Application forthwith." "That Application" referenced in the writ was approved on May 17, 2021. Contrary to Petitioner's reading of the Court of Appeal's directions and this Court's Order on the Writ of Mandate the directive "to conduct any other proceeding consistent with the views expressed in this Court of Appeal's opinion" were addressed to this Court (i.e. "directing this Court to (1) grant and (2) to conduct ." No argument is made and no argument is provided that there was any delay in the approval of the application by the Department of Public Works on May 17, 2021, about 20 days after the service of the writ of mandate. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re: Defendant'S Legal Representation And Plaintiff'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2022 line 9. OSC RE: DEFENDANT'S LEGAL REPRESENTATION AND PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT is CONTINUED in part and DENIED in part. OSC Re: Defendant's Legal Representation is continued to June 10, 2022 per March 3, 2022 Order. Motion for Entry of Judgment for breach of stipulation is DENIED. Defendant to make timely payments going forward. Plaintiff to confirm that the online portal is accessible by 5:00 p.m. on March 14, 2022 via e-mail to Defendant's counsel. Defendant to confirm that she successfully was able to log into the online portal no later than 8:00 a.m. on March 15, 2022 via e-mail to her counsel. rulings.Case Title: ESSEX PROTFOLIO, L.P. VS. NANCY C. HAFERMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT ASHLEY BROWNE'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required. At the hearing claimant to present (1) a copy of the rental agreement referenced in the March 10, 2022 Claim of Right to Possession and proof of rental payments prior to July 29, 2021; and (2) any evidence of occupancy prior to July 29, 2021, e.g. driver's license showing the address of the premises, any communication with governmental agencies directed to the claimant at the premises, utility bills, etc. All evidence that claimant intends to present to the Court must be first provided to the Plaintiff. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANITA A. EVERT TRUSTEE OF THE ANITA A. EVERT VS. ARIS DE LOS SANTOS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 15, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT EVANGELINA FADRIGO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED on uncertainty grounds only. The operative notice, inter alia, is based on CCP 1161(3) governing termination of tenancy based on breach of a lease. Complaint does not allege existence of a lease and does not attach one. Plaintiff shall have a 5-day leave to amend to reconcile the allegations: if this UD is based on CCP 1161(3), to allege the existence of the breached lease and the provisions of the lease breached, if lease is written, to attach a copy of the lease. If there is no lease, Plaintiff shall allege in good faith other facts supporting termination based on CCP 1161(3). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
